title: hopea anomala
intro: Hopea anomala, also known as the Borneo ironwood, is a large tree species native to Borneo and the Philippines. The medicinal properties of Hopea anomala are attributed to its heartwood, which is rich in a lignan called vateria lignan, that has been shown to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Hopea anomala include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Hopea anomala typically involve the extraction of its heartwood, which can be found in the form of supplements, teas, and traditional remedies in some parts of Southeast Asia.
